definition: Wholesome thinking of renunciation in the MN 19 twofold division.
notes: Use this record in MN 19-style analytical and practical contexts where wholesome thinking is explicitly classified. Keep the compound tied to nekkhamma as freeing renunciation and to vitakka as active thinking rather than passive occurrence.

example_phrases.notes: First member of the wholesome class in MN 19.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use for MN 19-style ethical analysis of wholesome renunciation-oriented thinking.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the compound into a single thought-event or let renunciation drift into self-denial language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the compound from losing either the active thinking force or the freeing sense of renunciation.
